sections.headline: 🔎 GCC Intelligence Signals
sections.summary: US Federal Reserve holds rates at 4.25-4.50%, with GCC central banks expected to maintain currency peg alignment. Brent crude trades near $78/bbl as OPEC+ extends 2.2M bpd voluntary cuts. GCC banking sector shows robust NIM expansion; digital banking adoption reaches 65% in KSA and 72% in UAE.
sections.insight: The macro backdrop remains supportive for GCC financials, with higher-for-longer rates boosting lending income and oil prices stabilizing near $78.

sections.headline: Fed Holds Rates; GCC Central Banks Align
sections.summary: The US Federal Reserve held rates at 4.25-4.50%, and GCC central banks (SAMA, UAE CB, QCB) are expected to follow suit to maintain currency peg alignment. This supports continued NIM expansion for GCC banks like SNB, QNB, and FAB.
sections.insight: The higher-for-longer rate environment reinforces the attractiveness of GCC banking stocks, with strong quarterly metrics already reported.

sections.headline: OPEC+ Extends Cuts; Brent Near $78
sections.summary: OPEC+ maintains cautious output strategy with 2.2M bpd voluntary cuts extended, balancing market share discipline against global demand slowdown. Brent crude trades near $78/bbl.
sections.insight: The extension signals OPEC+'s commitment to price stability, supporting GCC fiscal revenues and Aramco's dividend yield of 6.2%.

sections.headline: GCC Banking Sector Sees NIM Expansion
sections.summary: GCC banking sector shows robust net interest margin (NIM) expansion as higher-for-longer rate environment boosts lending income. SNB, QNB, and FAB report strong quarterly metrics.
sections.insight: This sector rotation away from hydrocarbon-dependent assets toward financials indicates structural market deepening.

sections.headline: Aramco Shares Stabilize at SAR 28.50
sections.summary: Saudi Aramco shares stabilize around SAR 28.50 as sustained dividend yield of 6.2% attracts institutional flows despite moderate oil price environment.
sections.insight: Aramco's stability anchors the Saudi market, attracting institutional capital and supporting the Tadawul's liquidity.

sections.headline: GCC-China Trade Exceeds $350B; Yuan Contracts Gain
sections.summary: GCC-China trade volumes exceed $350B annually with yuan-denominated oil contracts gaining traction, signaling de-dollarization momentum in energy trade.
sections.insight: This shift toward multipolar trade relationships enhances GCC economic sovereignty and reduces dependency on Western financial systems.

sections.headline: GCC Fintech Scales AI-Driven Services
sections.summary: GCC fintech sector deploys AI-driven credit scoring, fraud detection, and robo-advisory at scale. Digital banking adoption reaches 65% in KSA and 72% in UAE.
sections.insight: This sovereign digital infrastructure push signals strategic intent to establish digital independence from external technology dependencies.

sections.headline: Saudi Arabia Commits $15B to NEOM AI City
sections.summary: Saudi Arabia accelerates NEOM AI City and data center investments with $15B committed to GPU clusters and sovereign AI infrastructure buildout.
sections.insight: The massive investment underscores Vision 2030's focus on AI as a pillar of economic diversification, positioning KSA as a regional tech hub.
